patch_type: Advisor-demo show-readiness cleanup over V60.3.1 Runtime Evidence Panel Correction
release_chain_status: V60.3.2 is the current implemented build. It adds concise workspace framing, reduces duplicated scaffolding, demotes supporting detail, and preserves the corrected Evidence/Runtime/Proof Pack information architecture. GitHub Pages browser QA is required after deploy.
top_level_tab_rule: Do not add more top-level tabs. Upgrade existing workspaces only.
public_naming_rule: Public product name is CyberShield Executive OS. Current prototype build is V60.3.2. Do not call the public build CyberShield OS v8 unless the repo, README, loader, Settings/admin metadata, and public UX intentionally move to that version scheme.
controlling_doctrine: No score without a model. No model without evidence. No evidence without traceability. No action without consequence.
show_readiness_rule: CyberShield should be clean enough to show advisors, prospects, experts, and potential pilot users without making them sort through accumulated build scaffolding.
information_architecture_rule: Evidence tab owns the full Manual Evidence Workbench. Runtime tab owns compact evidence requirements for action. Proof Pack tab owns proof-oriented evidence trace and defensibility context.
runtime_doctrine: Runtime should feel like command authority, not a junk drawer. It should show what is blocking, constraining, escalating, or allowing the action now.
model_maturity_rule: CyberShield scoring models are Expert-Derived V1 Trust Scoring Models, not Statistically Validated Trust Models. Default weights are calibratable and not authoritative until validated through test scenarios, expert review, red-team attempts, and real-world use cases.
v60_3_2_changes: adds show-ready workspace framing to Briefing, TrustMap, Runtime, Evidence, Proof Pack, Architecture, and Settings
preserves Evidence as the full Manual Evidence Workbench workspace
preserves Runtime as the compact Runtime Evidence Requirements workspace
preserves Proof Pack as the proof-oriented evidence trace workspace
demotes repeated supporting detail where it risks visual clutter
adds workspace focus notes for Runtime, Evidence, and Proof Pack
preserves V60.3 Universal Model Trace Inspector

show_readiness_cleanup.boundary: Static advisory cleanup pass. It changes presentation, framing, and section emphasis only. It does not add live scoring, evidence retrieval, backend persistence, ticketing, notifications, workflow automation, or enforcement.

trust_evidence_workbench.boundary: Static advisory prototype only. No live evidence retrieval, backend persistence, ticketing, notifications, workflow automation, or enforcement.

universal_model_trace_inspector.boundary: Static advisory prototype only. The registry defines expert-derived V1 calibratable models. It does not perform live scoring, live evidence retrieval, statistical validation, certification, legal determination, workflow automation, ticketing, notification, backend persistence, or enforcement.

recommended_next_build.label: V60.3.3 Advisor Demo Polish and Feedback Kit
goals: reduce remaining clutter after browser QA
prepare CyberShield Executive OS for first outside feedback demos
preserve no-new-top-level-tabs and prototype boundary language

implemented_release_train.v56: Trust Model Registry and Score Explanation Layer
implemented_release_train.v59.5: Internet Trust QA and Copy Guardrail Pass

prototype_boundary: The current GitHub Pages build is a lightweight static advisory prototype. It is not connected to live enterprise telemetry, SIEM, EDR, IAM, Microsoft 365, cloud control planes, GRC tooling, production agent enforcement, Google Sheets, CRM sync, ticketing, notifications, marketplace systems, ad platforms, domain-intelligence systems, identity verification systems, CMMC certification systems, healthcare compliance validation systems, banking/payment systems, live monitoring, live URL ingestion, live internet retrieval, live evidence retrieval, live scoring, live claim extraction, internet claim verification, report delivery, ticketing, workflow automation, notifications, backend persistence, statistical validation, or takedown automation.
known_limitations: Cleanup pass demotes clutter but does not perform browser QA
Runtime panel does not perform live workflow actions
Evidence workbench does not persist to a backend
Models are expert-derived V1 and not statistically validated
Default weights are calibratable and not authoritative
No live scoring, evidence retrieval, ticketing, notifications, or workflow automation
GitHub Pages browser QA required after deploy
